current: Let A_alpha=(1+alpha)/(2+alpha) and kappa=alpha/(2+2 alpha). Suppose a Pi-indexed estimator pi_tilde obeys a self-localized basic inequality R_P(pi_tilde) <= 2 Z_n(R_P(pi_tilde)) + delta_n, where Z_n(r) is a nondecreasing process satisfying E_P Z_n(r) <= C B n^(-1/2) r^kappa (log n)^p for every r. Then E_P R_P(pi_tilde) <= C{(B^2/n)^{A_alpha}(log n)^p + delta_n}. If delta_n<=1/n, the delta_n term is absorbed into the same rate.
proposed: Let A_alpha=(1+alpha)/(2+alpha) and kappa=alpha/(2+2 alpha). Let z_pi be a centered Pi-indexed process and Z_n(r)=sup_{pi in Pi:R_P(pi)<=r}|z_pi|. Suppose a Pi-indexed estimator pi_tilde obeys R_P(pi_tilde) <= 2 Z_n(R_P(pi_tilde)) + delta_n. Suppose also that, for rho_n=(B^2/n)^{A_alpha}(log n)^p with B>=1, the localized offset-peeling condition E_P sup_{pi in Pi}{2|z_pi|-R_P(pi)/4}_+ <= C rho_n holds. Then E_P R_P(pi_tilde) <= C{rho_n+delta_n}. If delta_n<=1/n, the delta_n term is absorbed into the same rate.
reason: The current fixed-radius expectation hypothesis alone is too weak for a random-radius self-bound: an arbitrary monotone process can have small E Z_n(r) at each deterministic r but rare large paths that force E R_P(pi_tilde) of order B n^{-1/2}, not the fast fixed-point rate. The localized VC machinery supplies the standard offset-peeling positive-part control, and the proof uses exactly that stronger process condition.
